SEARCH
Package server

Class RequestParser

java.lang.Object
server.RequestParser
public class RequestParser extends Object
Utility parser engine designed to decode incoming HTTP request protocol streams into structured, type-safe RequestParser.RequestInfo data objects.

Dynamic HTTP Decoding Logic

The parser processes network TCP/IP byte streams sequentially according to HTTP standards:

  1. Request Line Parsing: Extracts the HTTP verb (e.g., "GET", "POST"), the full URI, and splits path segments and URI query-string key/value parameters (e.g., ?topic=A&message=5).
  2. Header Block Evaluation: Processes headers line by line until an empty line is reached. It searches specifically for the Content-Length header to determine the size of any attached body content.
  3. Defensive Body Retrieval: Reads request body data only if Content-Length is greater than zero. It checks BufferedReader.ready() before reading bytes to prevent execution threads from blocking indefinitely on active sockets.

Required Core Framework Components (Internal Dependencies):

  • RequestParser.RequestInfo - Inner data container representing the parsed request context.

Code Integration Example:


 BufferedReader socketReader = new BufferedReader(new InputStreamReader(clientSocket.getInputStream()));
 
 // Decode the raw network stream into a structured RequestInfo entity
 RequestInfo info = RequestParser.parseRequest(socketReader);
 
 if (info != null) {
     System.out.println("HTTP Command: " + info.getHttpCommand());
     System.out.println("Requested URI: " + info.getUri());
     System.out.println("Target Parameter value: " + info.getParameters().get("topic"));
 }

  • Method Details

    • parseRequest

      public static RequestParser.RequestInfo parseRequest(BufferedReader reader) throws IOException
      Parses the incoming HTTP request stream and maps its components into a structured RequestInfo object.

      Processes the request line, path segments, query parameters, header blocks, and reads body payloads defensively, checking socket buffer readiness to prevent indefinite blocking.

      Parameters:
      reader - the buffered character stream reader linked to the client connection socket
      Returns:
      a decoded, structured RequestInfo container, or null if the stream is empty or invalid
      Throws:
      IOException - if a network reading error occurs during execution
